title: PCR-based cytotyping to detect Wolbachia in insect tissues
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"4esnf","text":"This protocol details a PCR-based method for detecting Wolbachia in insect tissues. By amplifying the wsp gene, specific to Wolbachia, and the host 28s rDNA gene as a control, this technique allows for accurate identification of Wolbachia-bearing and Wolbachia-free samples. Proper interpretation of control samples is crucial to ensure reliable results.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":55,"length":9},{"style":"italic","offset":102,"length":3},{"style":"italic","offset":124,"length":9},{"style":"italic","offset":229,"length":9},{"style":"italic","offset":251,"length":9}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"8ivl9","text":"Unless otherwise specified, steps are performed at room temperature in a 1.5 mL PCR-clean Eppendorf tube containing the appropriate tissue. Precautions are taken throughout to protect samples against contamination. All centrifuge steps are at 12,000 x g.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"fq6pg","text":"Sterilize the working surface with 10% bleach and 70% ethanol.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"9p482","text":"This protocol involves the use of various chemicals and reagents that require careful handling and strict adherence to safety guidelines to ensure safe laboratory practices. Please review the Material Safety Data Sheets (MSDS) for each reagent before beginning the protocol and take appropriate precautions. All steps should be performed while wearing gloves, a lab coat, and eye protection.","type":"align-justify","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Centrifuge for 00:02:00 to pellet cellular debris.

Create two PCR master mixes with a final reaction volume of 10 µL with 2 µL of DNA template. Each sample should be tested with wsp primers (for Wolbachia ) and 28s (for the host). wsp_F: 5'-TGGTCCAATAAGTGATGAAGAAAC-3' wsp_R: 5'-AAAAATTAAACGCTACTCCA-3' 28s_F: 5'-TACCGTGAGGGAAAGTTGAAA-3' 28s_R: 5'-AGACTCCTTGGTCCGTGTTT-3' Plan for the inclusion of a positive control known to have Wolbachia , a negative control without Wolbachia , and a no template control where water is added in place of a DNA template.
Collect samples by adding three ceramic beads (2.8 mm) and flies to a 1.5 mL centrifuge tube
Incubate at 65 °C for 00:45:00 at 300 rpm, °C, to lyse cells using a ThermoMixer.
Incubate at 94 °C for 00:04:00 at 300 rpm, °C, to inactivate Proteinase K.

Store squish-extracted DNA at -20 °C for 2 weeks or at 4 °C for 24 hours. Considerations for use: Centrifuge the DNA before use to bring any protein/fly pieces to the bottom of the tube. When pipetting, take care to only collect the liquid on the top of the mixture.
Add 8 µL of the master mix to each well.
Add 2 µL of the DNA template to each well.
Cap or seal wells. Centrifuge for 00:00:10 at 12000 rcf, °C, . Transfer to a thermocycler.

Perform PCR following the relevant program. wsp program: 94°C for 5 min, (94°C for 30 sec, 55°C for 30 sec, and 72°C for 75 sec)x40, 72°C for 5 min, hold at 4°C 28s program: 94°C for 2 min, (94°C for 30 sec, 50°C for 30 sec, and 72°C for 60 sec)x40, 72°C for 8 min, hold at 4°C
Seal gel tray in preparation of adding liquid agarose.
Add 100 mL 1x LB Buffer to a 250 mL Erlenmeyer flask. Add 1 g agarose to the flask. Swirl to mix.

Microwave until just boiling. Stop the microwave. Swirl the flask to mix. Look for flakes of unmelted agarose. Continue until fully dissolved. Caution: The glass will be hot, and the agarose can boil over if not observant.
Run tap water over the flask for 00:00:30 to cool. Swirl the flask while cooling.
Add 2 drops of ethidium bromide to the flask and mix well by swirling. Pour liquid agarose gel into the gel tray and add comb(s). Be careful not to create bubbles in the gel with the comb.
Wait 00:20:00 for the gel to solidify
Carefully remove the combs and unseal the gel tray.
Load gel and tray into gel box. Add 1x LB Buffer until the fill line.
Add 3 µL of the ladder into the furthest left well on each row.
Add 3 µL of each PCR sample into each well. Work quickly or the samples will start to disperse in the wells. Be careful not to poke the pipet tip into the side or bottom of the well.
Cover the gel box and attach it to a power supply. Run the gel at 300 Volts. Monitor the migration of the loading dyes to determine when to stop.
Image the gel.
Add 50 µL of squish buffer per Drosophila fruit fly.

Mix the following in a 500 mL glass screw-top jar. Use a graduated cylinder to measure liquids and an analytical balance to measure solids. 20 mL Tris-HCl ( 1 Molarity (M) , pH = 8.0) 0.0744 g EDTA 0.2922 g NaCl 180 mL diH2O
Autoclave 00:30:00 on fluid cycle to sterilize. Make sure the screw top is loose. Allow to cool to room temperature.
Add 300 µL of Proteinase K. Mix by gently shaking the bottle.
Create 15 mL aliquots labeled with the date.
Store at 4 o C until use.
Homogenize flies for 00:02:00 at 1500 rpm, °C, using a bead-mill homogenizer.

Interpret the control samples. If the controls are as expected, proceed to interpret the experimental samples. Interpreting no-template control (NTC): Expected result: No bands for wsp or 28s. Unexpected result: Any band indicates reagent contamination. Cytotyping inconclusive. Interpreting Wolbachia -free DNA (negative control): Expected result: No wsp band, clear 28s band. Unexpected result: wsp band indicates sample contamination during collection or processing. No 28s band indicates poor DNA quality, PCR issues, or errors in sample loading. Cytotyping inconclusive. Interpreting Wolbachia -containing DNA (positive control): Expected result: Clear bands for both wsp and 28s. Unexpected result: No bands indicate poor DNA quality, PCR issues, or errors in sample loading. Cytotyping inconclusive. Interpreting test samples: + wsp , + 28s: Wolbachia -positive - wsp , + 28s: Wolbachia -negative + wsp , - 28s: Potentially Wolbachia -positive, but technical or human errors may have affected the 28s reaction. - wsp , - 28s: Inconclusive. Technical or human errors likely impacted both reactions.
creator: J. Dylan Shropshire
